description: CSS-native DOM for visual layout plus a minimal anchor API that reports live DOM bounds for cursor-attach. Browser computes layout; API only reports. ObjectStateChange remains state-only and does not call the anchor API.

production_files_touched.change: one conditional gate scoped to well_plate_96_zoom plus feature flag; returns empty ComputedItemLayout[] for the spike scene
new module exposing 2-3 anchor read functions over the live DOM

anchor_api.bail_threshold: more than 3 functions, OR any input other than a string name/id, OR any read outside the live DOM, OR any per-frame invocation

cursor_attach.approach: state-only mutation unchanged; cursor-follower chrome calls getAnchorForObject once at attach time to pin ghost; per-frame position from native pointermove
cursor_attach.production_changes: none in apply.ts; chrome layer reads new anchor module

object_state_change.approach: no anchor API call; existing apply.ts state swap unchanged; re-render updates DOM class/style; browser re-runs CSS layout

adapter_return.fallback: synthetic single-entry layout populated by one-shot getAnchorBoundsForObject call

precheck.notes: precheck inspects rendered DOM; anchor API does not perturb precheck output

risks.risk: anchor API grows beyond a read-only DOM bridge
risks.threshold: more than 3 functions, OR a 4th function whose name encodes intent (getAnchorBetween, placeAnchorRelativeTo, solveAnchorChain), OR any input other than a name/id string, OR any read outside the live DOM
risks.action_on_breach: stop; document; bail per packet failure conditions

risks.risk: per-frame anchor reads turn the module into a tracker
risks.threshold: any caller invokes the anchor API inside an animation frame, scroll handler, or pointermove handler
risks.action_on_breach: stop; route follow-up via native pointer events, not anchor reads

risks.risk: synthetic ComputedItemLayout coordinates start being consumed for layout intent
risks.threshold: a consumer outside render/scene.ts or well_plate/render.ts reads layout.x or layout.y
risks.action_on_breach: stop; consumer either no-ops for the spike scene or is rerouted to the anchor API directly

compatibility_notes: click dispatch already reads data-target-id from the live DOM via closest() at src/scene_runtime/dispatch/click.ts lines 29-79; CSS-native DOM emitting the same attributes inherits the existing pipeline with zero changes
CursorAttach applier at src/scene_runtime/render/apply.ts lines 149-198 is state-only and is not changed by Path C
ObjectStateChange applier at src/scene_runtime/render/apply.ts lines 31-111 is state-only and is not changed by Path C
recommendation_rationale: lowest TS-side complexity among the three paths because no layout math runs in TypeScript and no render-side code is touched; the only spike question is whether a one-shot anchor read at attach time is enough for cursor-attach, which is answerable by the existing walker without rebuilding any subsystem
one_sentence_risk_summary: Path C silently becomes a layout engine if the anchor API grows past 3 read-only DOM functions or starts taking layout intent (zone, region, align, offset) as input.
